books.description: A man travels to a small Southern town, where he purchases pencil drawings of local residents and exchanges them for stories.

books.description: Natalie Heller Mills, a privileged tradwife social media influencer, wakes up to find she is living in the comparatively difficult reality of 1855.

books.description: Decades after their time together, a woman reconnects with her stepfather and they look back at the directions their lives have taken.

books.description: Ryland Grace awakes from a long sleep alone and far from home, and the fate of humanity rests on his shoulders; the basis of the movie.

books.description: The 25th book in the Scot Harvath series. America’s top spy investigates bombings that may portend a shake up in the geopolitical balance.

books.description: Hannah Wells makes a deal with the captain of the college hockey team in exchange for tutoring him that may complicate their relationship.

books.description: Natalie Heller Mills, a privileged tradwife social media influencer, wakes up to find she is living in the comparatively difficult reality of 1855. Read by Rebecca Lowman. 13 hours, 47 minutes unabridged.
books.title: YESTERYEAR

books.author: Andy Weir
books.description: The fate of humanity rests on Ryland Grace's shoulders; the basis of the movie. Read by Ray Porter. 16 hours, 10 minutes unabridged.
books.title: PROJECT HAIL MARY

books.author: Matt Dinniman
books.description: A Coast Guard vet named Carl and his ex-girlfriend’s cat, Princess Donut, are trapped in a fantasy dungeon. Read by Jeff Hays. 13 hours, 31 minutes unabridged.
books.title: DUNGEON CRAWLER CARL

books.author: Allen Levi
books.description: A man travels to a small Southern town, where he purchases pencil drawings of local residents and exchanges them for stories. Read by David Morse. 13 hours, 12 minutes unabridged.
books.title: THEO OF GOLDEN

books.author: Elle Kennedy
books.description: Hannah Wells makes a deal with the captain of the college hockey team in exchange for tutoring him that may complicate their relationship. Read by Christian Fox and Lorelei Avalon. 12 hours, 25 minutes unabridged.
books.title: THE DEAL

books.author: Matt Dinniman
books.description: The second book in the Dungeon Crawler Carl series. Read by Jeff Hays. 11 hours, 28 minutes unabridged.
books.title: CARL'S DOOMSDAY SCENARIO

books.author: Matt Dinniman
books.description: The third book in the Dungeon Crawler Carl series. Read by Jeff Hays and the Critical Drinker. 16 hours, 54 minutes unabridged.
books.title: THE DUNGEON ANARCHIST'S COOKBOOK

books.author: Jack Carr and M.P. Woodward
books.contributor: by Jack Carr and M.P. Woodward
books.description: The former Navy SEAL and C.I.A. ground branch operative Chris Walker doles out vigilante justice. Read by Ray Porter. 13 hours, 36 minutes unabridged.
books.title: THE FOURTH OPTION

books.author: Kathryn Stockett
books.description: As things get tougher during the Depression, several women look for ways to improve their circumstances. Read by Jenna Lamia and January LaVoy. 28 hours, 44 minutes unabridged.
books.title: THE CALAMITY CLUB

books.author: Virginia Evans
books.description: Letters from someone she used to know push Sybil Van Antwerp toward revisiting her past and finding a way to forgive. Read by Maggi-Meg Reed, Jane Oppenheimer et al. 8 hours, 36 minutes unabridged.
books.title: THE CORRESPONDENT

books.author: Shelby Van Pelt
books.description: A widow working the night shift at an aquarium is aided in solving a mystery by a giant octopus; the basis of the movie. Read by Marin Ireland and Michael Urie. 11 hours, 16 minutes unabridged.
books.title: REMARKABLY BRIGHT CREATURES

books.author: Matt Dinniman
books.description: The fifth book in the Dungeon Crawler Carl series. Read by Jeff Hays. 23 hours, 33 minutes unabridged.

books.author: Matt Dinniman
books.description: The seventh book in the Dungeon Crawler Carl series. Read by Jeff Hays and Travis Baldree. 28 hours, 40 minutes unabridged.
books.title: THIS INEVITABLE RUIN

books.author: Elle Kennedy
books.description: The second book in the Off-Campus series. A college hockey player attempts to redeem himself to a rising sophomore. Read by Lorelei Avalon and Lee Samuels. 10 hours, 11 minutes unabridged.
books.title: THE MISTAKE

books.author: Belle Burden
books.description: Burden retraces her marriage of 20 years in search of clues to help shape her understanding about its demise and to find a way forward. Read by the author. 7 hours, 4 minutes unabridged.
books.title: STRANGERS

books.author: Lena Dunham
books.description: The author of “Not That Kind of Girl” evaluates the effects that pursuing her creative endeavors had on her. Read by the author. 11 hours, 47 minutes unabridged.
books.title: FAMESICK

books.author: Gad Saad
books.description: The host of the podcast “The Saad Truth” argues that what he considers misguided compassion is having a negative impact on society. Read by the author. 8 hours, 23 minutes unabridged.

books.author: Patrick Radden Keefe
books.description: The author of “Say Nothing” details the efforts by the parents of a 19-year-old Londoner to uncover the truth about his mysterious death and secret life. Read by the author. 13 hours unabridged.
books.title: LONDON FALLING

books.author: David Sedaris
books.description: Essays on the passage of time, complicated relationships and some unexpected delights life has to offer. Read by the author. 7 hours, 26 minutes unabridged.

books.author: Bessel van der Kolk
books.description: How trauma affects the body and mind, and innovative treatments for recovery. Read by Sean Pratt. 16 hours, 17 minutes unabridged.
books.title: THE BODY KEEPS THE SCORE

books.author: Hayden Panettiere
books.description: The Golden Globe-nominated actress describes how she contended with fame and handled difficulties in her life. Read by the author. 8 hours, 47 minutes unabridged.

books.description: The Emmy Award-winning actress, who received a multiple sclerosis diagnosis in 2021, shares personal stories. Read by the author. 8 hours, 27 minutes unabridged.
books.title: YOU WITH THE SAD EYES

books.description: The author of “Where Law Ends” argues that a flaw in America’s legal system allows for deceit in our politics. Read by the author. 4 hours, 31 minutes unabridged.
